description_long: This object consists of two irregularly shaped stones, one slightly larger and more rounded, the other smaller and more angular. Both stones have a matte, rough surface texture, with subtle color differences: the larger stone is a cool bluish-gray, while the smaller one is a pale, almost white-gray. The stones appear solid and heavy, with no visible cracks, carvings, or artificial modifications. Their surfaces are mostly smooth with gentle undulations, lacking sharp edges or pronounced facets. The stones are positioned side by side, suggesting they could serve as natural barriers or decorative elements in an urban landscape. There are no visible signs of significant weathering, such as moss, lichen, or deep erosion, indicating they may have been recently placed or minimally exposed to the elements.
description: A pair of solid, irregularly shaped stones—one bluish-gray and rounded, the other pale gray and more angular—with rough, matte surfaces and no visible weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing both stones side by side, highlighting their size difference and color contrast.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the depth and rounded profile of the larger stone, with the smaller stone partially obscured behind.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the broader face of the larger stone and the squarer aspect of the smaller stone.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite direction, showing the angularity of the smaller stone and the sloped back of the larger stone.
attribute_location: urban park, sidewalk edge, or landscaped plaza
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with other stones on the ground surface
attribute_pattern: No distinct patterns; surfaces are mostly uniform with subtle natural mottling and minor undulations.
attribute_weathering: Surfaces are smooth and matte, with minimal visible weathering or aging.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts gently with paved urban surfaces; may be partially surrounded by grass or mulch.
